raw:
Let me look for any frontend source files that might provide insight into the UI implementation.
```json
{
  "criterion": "ui_ux",
  "score": 1,
  "evidence": [
    "lighthouse-summary.json shows pages load at http://localhost:5173/books (perf 0.76, a11y 0.92), /checkout (perf 0.86, a11y 0.95), /members (perf 0.80, a11y 0.95) — confirming pages exist and render at a basic level",
    "axe-summary.json reports 'skipped: no axe spec in candidate' — no accessibility audit results",
    "frontend-tree.txt shows component files exist: BookList, BookDetail, MemberList, MemberDetail, Checkout, Modal, ConfirmReturnModal, PayFineModal, Layout, global.css — but none of their source code is included in evidence",
    "file-sizes.csv shows Checkout.tsx (191 lines), MemberDetail.tsx (219 lines), Modal.tsx (45 lines), ConfirmReturnModal.tsx (19 lines), PayFineModal.tsx (19 lines) — suggesting some component logic exists",
    "No screenshots of any page in any state (empty, populated, error) are present — empty screenshots/ directory",
    "No screen captures of the 3-step checkout flow are present",
    "No capture of any modal interaction (return confirmation or pay-fine) is present",
    "No rendered HTML of representative pages (catalog, member profile) is present",
    "No frontend component source code, CSS, or styling is included in evidence to assess visual hierarchy, affordance, form quality, error states, empty states, or loading indicators"
  ],
  "reasoning": "The rubric requires screenshots, screen captures, and rendered HTML as primary evidence to assess UI/UX quality. None of these are present — the screenshots/ directory is empty. The only available evidence is Lighthouse performance/accessibility scores confirming pages exist and load, and a file structure listing showing which components exist. Without any visual or interactive evidence (screenshots, HTML, captures, component source), none of the rubric criteria above level 1 can be verified. The app cannot be confirmed to have visual hierarchy, clear affordances, proper empty/error states, form validation, step indicators, modals with focus trapping, loading states, or consistent patterns. Score capped at 1 per rubric guidance: 'Pages render but core flows are broken or confusing enough that a new librarian could not complete a checkout without instruction' — we cannot confirm any flow is usable since zero interaction/visual evidence was provided."
}
```

evidence: lighthouse-summary.json shows pages load at http://localhost:5173/books (perf 0.76, a11y 0.92), /checkout (perf 0.86, a11y 0.95), /members (perf 0.80, a11y 0.95) — confirming pages exist and render at a basic level
axe-summary.json reports 'skipped: no axe spec in candidate' — no accessibility audit results

frontend-tree.txt shows component files exist: BookList, BookDetail, MemberList, MemberDetail, Checkout, Modal, ConfirmReturnModal, PayFineModal, Layout, global.css — but none of their source code is included in evidence

file-sizes.csv shows Checkout.tsx (191 lines), MemberDetail.tsx (219 lines), Modal.tsx (45 lines), ConfirmReturnModal.tsx (19 lines), PayFineModal.tsx (19 lines) — suggesting some component logic exists
No screenshots of any page in any state (empty, populated, error) are present — empty screenshots/ directory
No screen captures of the 3-step checkout flow are present
No capture of any modal interaction (return confirmation or pay-fine) is present
No rendered HTML of representative pages (catalog, member profile) is present
No frontend component source code, CSS, or styling is included in evidence to assess visual hierarchy, affordance, form quality, error states, empty states, or loading indicators
parsed.reasoning: The rubric requires screenshots, screen captures, and rendered HTML as primary evidence to assess UI/UX quality. None of these are present — the screenshots/ directory is empty. The only available evidence is Lighthouse performance/accessibility scores confirming pages exist and load, and a file structure listing showing which components exist. Without any visual or interactive evidence (screenshots, HTML, captures, component source), none of the rubric criteria above level 1 can be verified. The app cannot be confirmed to have visual hierarchy, clear affordances, proper empty/error states, form validation, step indicators, modals with focus trapping, loading states, or consistent patterns. Score capped at 1 per rubric guidance: 'Pages render but core flows are broken or confusing enough that a new librarian could not complete a checkout without instruction' — we cannot confirm any flow is usable since zero interaction/visual evidence was provided.
